title: Flaky Korean Garlic Namul
description: A simple, nostalgic Korean dish from Busan, made with garlic bulb, lacquer-glaze, and perfect for a whole30 diet. The flaky texture and rich flavor will transport you to the coastal city.
instructions: Peel the garlic bulbs and separate the cloves.
Mince the garlic cloves using a chef's knife or a garlic press.
In a small saucepan, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until fragrant and lightly browned.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and water.
Add the soy sauce mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Stir in the sesame seeds and cornstarch.
Add the garlic mixture to a mortar and pestle or a bowl, and mash until the garlic is wellbroken down.
Season the garlic mixture with black pepper and salt to taste.
Shape the garlic mixture into small, round balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat a small amount of sesame oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the garlic balls to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Serve the Flaky Korean Garlic Namul warm, garnished with additional sesame seeds if desired.
Let the garlic balls cool completely on a wire rack.
getStore in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 5 days or in the freezer for up to 2 months.
Freeze the garlic balls on a baking sheet, then transfer them to an airtight container or freezer bag for storage.
